title: Container image podman v5.4.2 missing awk due to change to latest Fedora version
body:
### Feature request description

I have noticed with image quay.io/podman/stable:v5.4.2 that there is an awk missing due to this image having fedora:42 as a base image which, for some reason, is missing awk.

The previous version quay.io/podman/stable:v5.4.1 doesn't have that issue since it's using fedora:41 and that one still has awk.

The issue here isn't the lack of awk, which is presumably a question for fedora image maintainers. The issue is that the podman/stable image jumped PATCH version (in semver v2 terms which I'm not sure if this project is using) and I would definitely not expect the leap in major version of base image the podman/stable is using since such decision might introduce breaking changes.

### Suggest potential solution

Revisit the versioning of the podman/stable image and either raise the MINOR version and/or include the change from fedora:41 to fedora:42 in the release notes.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I don't mind changes but I just don't want to find out the hard way they happen, I believe wanting more detailed release notes about changes in between image versions is not that much to ask for.

### Additional context

I found out only because most of our pipelines for building images run in podman/stable image and some of them use awk, they stopped working all of a sudden. Using v5.4 tag which pulled us the v5.4.2 version yesterday and pipelines started failing.

v5.4 should mean that there are not breaking changes and even though inclusion of awk in the image might not be in the scope of this project, making sure users know about the change of the base image and potential outcomes should be.

comments.body:
> I don't mind changes but I just don't want to find out the hard way they happen, I believe wanting more detailed release notes about changes in between image versions is not that much to ask for.

It is, the image build process is totally automated. And the versions just match the podman versions one to one as they use the fedoras distro package to build.

> > I don't mind changes but I just don't want to find out the hard way they happen, I believe wanting more detailed release notes about changes in between image versions is not that much to ask for.
> 
> It is, the image build process is totally automated. And the versions just match the podman versions one to one as they use the fedoras distro package to build.

@Luap99  Can you point me to the release notes or other resource where I can see that there has been a version change from fedora:41 to fedora:42 between container image versions v5.4.1 and v5.4.2? If it was easy to find for me then I would not need to create this issue.
It sounds like the automated process of the container image build just uses latest tag of fedora, that doesn't sound like a good thing to do.

I don't know where the fedora container images changes are documented, if they are. My comment was meant to say us provided detailed releases notes for new image is a big ask.
(Also note, for us the actual podman release notes and container images are completely decoupled. The container images just installed the fedora packages which means there is always like a week or two delay until the package is in fedora so by the time the podman release notes are written we have no idea what will be in the container image)

Using the latest tag make sense for us as this is totally automated and nobody from us really has time to look into and update the https://github.com/containers/image_build repo all the time.

I have searched the web and several projects already reacted by adding awk to the dependencies. It'd be nice if podman image retained the awk installed if it is possible so people using the podman image do not have to rebuild it with awk.

I suggest you continue the conversation in https://github.com/containers/image_build/issues/45 as the images are build there, if enough people think awk must be in the image then there isn't really strong reason not to explicitly install it there I suppose.
